title: Deployment Strategist
must_haves: Strong background in customer-facing, ambiguous environments: solutions engineering / deployment strategy / consulting / customer engineering / operational transformation
Translate business problems into practical technical workflows
Comfortable discussing APIs, integrations, AI systems, deployment constraints with engineers
Drive adoption and organisational change (not just delivery)
hard_blockers: Core experience expectation is enterprise solutions-engineering / consulting / customer-engineering delivery — Ryan's is community-led growth, adjacent not core
Enterprise software / CFO-room deployments rather than crypto/Web3 community domain
met_strengths: Prompt-engineering / LLM-workflow familiarity (nice-to-have) is a genuine match via Claude Code agents
Strong ownership in ambiguous environments; founder-adjacent
Customer/community-facing trust-building and enablement (ambassador programmes, hackathons)
London-based, UK auth; salary band £38-65K is realistic
one_line_verdict: No hard-coding gate (it 'isn't a pure engineering role') and the LLM-workflow nice-to-haves fit Ryan well, but the core ask is enterprise solutions-engineering/consulting delivery experience he doesn't have — a credible stretch.
fetch_note: Confirmed open via Ashby API (job-board/solve.ai, slug bbfb4e8b), London, £38-65K. Full must-haves and nice-to-haves pulled from descriptionHtml.
